rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical multicomponent cascade of indole-tethered alkenes with CF3SO2Na and n-Bu4NI to assemble iodomethyl spiropyrrolidinyl-oxindoles via iodide-mediated radical trifluoromethylsulfonyloxylation, spirocyclization, and water coupling.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (I-) to I2 acts as the mediator that oxidizes CF3SO2Na to the CF3S(O)O• radical; additional anodic oxidations convert the spirocyclic C-radical to a cation and oxidize the hemiaminal to the oxindole carbonyl.

key_intermediates.name: Intermediate A: C-radical from O-addition of CF3S(O)O• to alkene of 1a
Intermediate B: spirocyclic indolyl C3-radical after 5-exo cyclization
Intermediate C: cationic indoleninium after anodic oxidation of B
Intermediate D: indolenine bearing CH2-OS(O)CF3 group
Intermediate E: sulfonic ester after second anodic oxidation of D
Intermediate 5: iodomethyl indolenine (SN2 product from E + I-)
Intermediate F: hemiaminal from H2O addition to 5
I2 (iodine, mediator from anodic oxidation of I-)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I2, which oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3S(O)O•; this O-centered radical adds to the tethered alkene (A), 5-exo-trig spirocyclization onto the indole gives C3-radical B, which is anodically oxidized to indoleninium C and deprotonated to indolenine D; a second anodic oxidation furnishes sulfonate ester E, SN2 displacement by I- yields iodomethyl indolenine 5, water addition gives hemiaminal F, and final anodic oxidation delivers the oxindole 3a.
rate_determining_step: Radical addition of CF3S(O)O• to the alkene via TS1 (ΔG‡ = 27.8 kcal/mol), the highest barrier on the computed profile.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a only in trace; TEMPO–SO2CF3 adduct 4 detected by ESI-MS, confirming CF3S(O)O• radical.

probe_reagents.name: Reaction without CF3SO2Na
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a, demonstrating CF3SO2Na is essential as the radical source.

probe_reagents.name: Reaction under N2 (no air)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a; suggests O2 or aerobic conditions contribute (control).

probe_reagents.name: N-alkyl indole substrates (1q, 1r; R = Me, Et)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% yield of spiroproduct, supporting indoleninium/indolenine intermediate requiring free N–H for tautomerization.

probe_reagents.name: Intermediate 5 detection (2 h reaction)
probe_reagents.observation: Iodomethyl indolenine intermediate 5 detected by ESI-MS, supporting proposed pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of n-Bu4NI, CF3SO2Na, 1a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak for n-Bu4NI at 0.71 V; CF3SO2Na at 1.90 V; no oxidation peak for 1a — reaction is triggered by iodide oxidation (mediator).

notes: Alternative 6-endo spirocyclization (TS2′, ΔG‡ = 25.6 kcal/mol) was computed but disfavored vs 5-exo TS2 (21.8 kcal/mol), explaining regioselectivity for the 5-membered spirocycle. An alternative direct SN2 on intermediate D by iodide was ruled out by DFT (TS3′ = 35.7 kcal/mol) in favor of prior anodic oxidation to sulfonic ester E followed by SN2 (TS3 = 26.0 kcal/mol). Direct CF3• incorporation (trifluoromethyl spiroproduct) was not observed — O- rather than C-radical addition operates.
